steps.title: Spot the paradox
steps.content: The decisive signal is the contradiction: CLPs have a shielding advantage in their transmission mechanism, yet their abundance decreased with rising temperatures, while directly transmitted parasites (without this shielding) remained stable.

steps.title: Resolve the contradiction
steps.content:
The matching choice explains why CLPs declined despite their shielding advantage: the transmission strategy's advantages “did not completely offset the negative effects of other temperature-driven factors.” This directly captures why better-protected parasites fared worse than exposed ones.

The other three either reverse the data (A and D) or assume transmission strategies the passage doesn't mention (B).

C is correct.
